evidence: Round ledger: round 1 statistics BLOCKED and execution BLOCKED; round 2 statistics NO_BLOCKER and execution BLOCKED; round 3 statistics NO_BLOCKER and execution NO_BLOCKER on commit 1d9b5dba before W4b; round 4 statistics NO_BLOCKER and execution NO_BLOCKER on commit a480cf07 after W4b.
Round 4 reviewed the exact 70-row manifest at SHA-256 4e06316aa35a95c85a330bf6d82a6ba87642f0d3e47a12810519c34f284b17a7 and found the cross-commit exporter-provenance defect resolved.
findings.summary: The four-round authorization ledger closes with dual NO_BLOCKER on the W4b manifest.

evidence: All five protected science files remain byte-identical to commit 6007d1d6.
Authority remains exactly rq014_g2_declassification_export plus rq014_g2_contract_preflight; resource pilot, G2R, G3R, G4R and rating access remain denied.
The required declassification_export_commit field adds provenance binding only and grants no data-access, rating-join, statistical or code-selection capability.
findings.summary: W4b preserves protected science bytes, rating blindness and the single-operation authority scope.

evidence: The persisted round-4 statistics report records 70 unique manifest rows with 70/70 hashes verified and no new defect.
No workspace, launcher or HPC action occurred; no rating value was accessed and no HPC job was submitted during statistics review.
findings.summary: The round-4 statistics review stayed inside the rating-blind, no-execution boundary.
